docs.title: Changelog
docs.text:
The project changelog is maintained in the repository root.

  • View CHANGELOG.md

docs.title: Getting Started
docs.text: KubeMemo is a standalone Kubedeck tool for attaching durable and runtime context to Kubernetes resources.

docs.title: What you get
docs.text:
• Durable memos for warnings, ownership, maintenance guidance, runbooks, and curated operational memory.
  • Runtime memos for incident context, temporary advice, and expiring breadcrumbs.
  • A native Go CLI and TUI.
  • A thin PowerShell wrapper module for PowerShell Gallery users.
  • GitOps-aware behavior for durable note workflows.

docs.title: Quick start
docs.text:
Install the cluster prerequisites:

Install-KubeMemo -EnableRuntimeStore -RuntimeNamespace kubememo-runtime

Create a durable memo:

New-KubeMemo `
  -Title "Orders API warm-up behavior" `
  -Summary "Expected transient 502s after deployment" `
  -Content "Ignore failures for up to 3 minutes after rollout." `
  -Kind Deployment `
  -Namespace prod `
  -Name orders-api `
  -NoteType warning

Read it back:

Show-KubeMemo -Kind Deployment -Namespace prod -Name orders-api
Open-KubeMemoTui

Start watching for captured activity:

Start-KubeMemoActivityCapture -Namespace prod -Kind Deployment

docs.title: Core model
docs.text:
• Durable memos live in memos.notes.kubememo.io
  • Runtime memos live in runtimememos.runtime.notes.kubememo.io
  • Durable and runtime notes are intentionally separate stores

That split matters most in GitOps clusters, where durable objects may be Git-managed but runtime notes still need safe live writes.

docs.title: Installation
docs.text:
KubeMemo has three supported installation paths:

  • Native CLI from Homebrew or GitHub Releases
  • PowerShell module from PowerShell Gallery
  • Optional Helm deployment for in-cluster activity capture

The native CLI is the core product experience. If you work primarily in PowerShell, KubeMemo is also available as a PowerShell module with PowerShell-friendly commands.

Use the path that best matches how your team works:

  • Native CLI installation
  • PowerShell installation
  • Helm activity-capture deployment

docs.title: Native CLI
docs.text:
Install with Homebrew or download a release asset, then verify the binary:

kubememo version --output json
kubememo status --output json

docs.title: PowerShell
docs.text:
Install from PowerShell Gallery:

Install-Module -Name KubeMemo -Repository PSGallery -Scope CurrentUser

docs.title: Cluster bootstrap
docs.text:
Whichever install path you choose, the next step is bootstrapping the cluster prerequisites:

Install-KubeMemo -EnableRuntimeStore -RuntimeNamespace kubememo-runtime

Install in GitOps-aware mode:

Install-KubeMemo -GitOpsAware -EnableRuntimeStore

Update installed prerequisites:

Update-KubeMemo -GitOpsAware

docs.title: Build locally
docs.text:
make build

Run the binary directly:

./KubeMemo/bin/$(go env GOOS)-$(go env GOARCH)/kubememo status --output json

docs.title: Prerequisites
docs.text:
• Access to a Kubernetes cluster
  • kubectl configured for that cluster
  • For PowerShell workflows: PowerShell 7+
  • For Helm workflows: Helm 3+
  • For docs development: MkDocs Material

docs.title: CLI Overview
docs.text: KubeMemo is now a Go-native CLI. The PowerShell module wraps this binary rather than re-implementing the product.

docs.title: Examples
docs.text:
kubememo get --namespace prod --output json
kubememo show --kind Deployment --namespace prod --name orders-api
kubememo watch-activity --namespace prod --kind Deployment
kubememo tui

docs.title: Human-facing defaults
docs.text:
• kubememo show includes runtime memos by default
  • kubememo find includes runtime memos by default
  • kubememo tui includes runtime memos by default

kubememo get remains durable-first because it is the more automation-oriented command.

docs.title: Output modes
docs.text:
• JSON for scripting
  • Memo-style card rendering for terminal reading
  • TUI for interactive browsing
  • Watch mode for auto-captured runtime activity memos

docs.title: PowerShell
docs.text: KubeMemo also ships as a PowerShell module for users who want PowerShell-native commands on top of the core CLI.

docs.title: What the PowerShell experience does
docs.text:
• Finds the bundled binary for the current OS and architecture
  • Passes through terminal commands for color and TUI support
  • Parses JSON results for object-returning commands
  • Preserves PowerShell-friendly command names and WhatIf/Confirm behavior

docs.title: Examples
docs.text:
Get-KubeMemo -Namespace prod
Show-KubeMemo -Kind Deployment -Namespace prod -Name orders-api
Open-KubeMemoTui
Get-KubeMemoVersion
Start-KubeMemoActivityCapture -Namespace prod -Kind Deployment

docs.title: Actor stamping
docs.text:
When possible, KubeMemo uses:

kubectl auth whoami -o json

That means CreatedBy and UpdatedBy reflect the RBAC-facing cluster identity rather than only the local shell user.

docs.title: TUI
docs.text: KubeMemo ships with an interactive terminal UI for browsing memos across namespaces, kinds, and runtime state.

docs.title: Launch
docs.text:
kubememo tui

PowerShell wrapper:

Open-KubeMemoTui

docs.title: Defaults
docs.text:
• Includes runtime memos by default
  • Auto-refreshes by default
  • Supports namespace, kind, and text filtering
  • Uses a split memo-board layout with detail pane rendering

docs.title: Navigation
docs.text:
[Arrows]/[j][k] move
[PgUp]/[PgDn] or [u][d] scroll
[/] text filter
[:] switch view
[f] namespace filter
[c] kind filter
[a] add temporary memo
[r] refresh
[q] quit

docs.title: Why the TUI exists
docs.text: The TUI is for discovery and incident-time reading. It complements the CLI rather than replacing it.

docs.title: Annotations
docs.text: KubeMemo can annotate target resources so kubectl describe shows that memo context exists.

docs.title: Annotation model
docs.text:
KubeMemo uses lightweight discovery annotations rather than storing full note bodies on the resource.

Typical keys:

  • notes.kubememo.io/enabled
  • notes.kubememo.io/has-note
  • notes.kubememo.io/note-count
  • notes.kubememo.io/runtime-count
  • notes.kubememo.io/summary
  • notes.kubememo.io/view

docs.title: Design rules
docs.text:
• Do not store full markdown memo content in annotations.
  • Prefer counts over listing every memo reference once a resource has many notes.
  • Only include a short summary when it is unambiguous.
  • Include a kubememo show ... command hint so the real source of truth is easy to open.

docs.title: Automatic behavior
docs.text: For direct writes in non-GitOps mode, KubeMemo can sync these annotations automatically for resource and namespace targets.

docs.title: kubectl Describe View
docs.text: KubeMemo annotations are designed to make kubectl describe more useful without dumping the full memo body into resource metadata.

docs.title: Example
docs.text:
After durable memos are attached to a Deployment, a kubectl describe can show lightweight memo metadata like:

Annotations:
  notes.kubememo.io/enabled:      true
  notes.kubememo.io/has-note:     true
  notes.kubememo.io/note-count:   3
  notes.kubememo.io/runtime-count: 1
  notes.kubememo.io/view:         kubememo show --kind Deployment --namespace prod --name orders-api

docs.title: Why this works
docs.text:
• operators immediately know memo context exists
  • the annotation stays small and readable
  • the view command points to the real source of truth
  • the resource is not bloated with full markdown note content

docs.title: Design principle
docs.text:
Annotations are for discoverability.

The actual durable and runtime memo content still lives in the KubeMemo CRDs, where it can be rendered properly in the CLI and TUI.

docs.title: GitOps Behavior
docs.text: KubeMemo is GitOps-aware.

docs.title: Standard clusters
docs.text:
• Direct durable writes allowed
  • Direct runtime writes allowed
  • Annotation sync can happen automatically on direct writes

docs.title: GitOps clusters
docs.text:
Durable notes should usually be treated as Git-managed. KubeMemo supports manifest/file generation for those workflows rather than assuming direct cluster mutation is always safe.

Runtime memos are only safe when the runtime store is outside GitOps reconciliation scope.

docs.title: Practical rule
docs.text:
• Durable store: curated source of truth
  • Runtime store: live operational context

Do not push temporary incident notes into durable Git-managed memo objects.

docs.title: Durable vs Runtime Memos
docs.text: KubeMemo uses a split-store model.

docs.title: Durable memos
docs.text:
• Resource: memos.notes.kubememo.io
  • Purpose: curated operational memory
  • Examples: ownership, warnings, runbooks, suppressions, maintenance notes

docs.title: Runtime memos
docs.text:
• Resource: runtimememos.runtime.notes.kubememo.io
  • Purpose: temporary or live operational context
  • Examples: incidents, activity breadcrumbs, short-lived advisories, handover notes

docs.title: Why the split matters
docs.text: In GitOps clusters, durable memos often belong in Git. Runtime memos need a separate non-reconciled live store so operators can still write temporary context without Git fighting those updates.

docs.title: macOS and Linux Installation
docs.text: KubeMemo is distributed as a native Go CLI for macOS and Linux.

docs.title: Homebrew
docs.text:
Once the Homebrew tap is updated, install with:

brew install KubeDeckio/tap/kubememo

Upgrade with:

brew upgrade kubememo

docs.title: GitHub Releases
docs.text:
You can also install directly from release assets on GitHub:

  1. Download the archive for your platform from the latest release.
  2. Extract kubememo.
  3. Move it somewhere on your PATH.

Example for macOS or Linux after extracting:

chmod +x kubememo
sudo mv kubememo /usr/local/bin/

docs.title: Verify the install
docs.text:
kubememo version --output json
kubememo status --output json

docs.title: Optional in-cluster activity capture with Helm
docs.text:
The Helm chart deploys the optional activity-capture component with secure defaults:

  • non-root container
  • dropped Linux capabilities
  • RuntimeDefault seccomp profile
  • read-only root filesystem
  • explicit CPU and memory requests and limits

Install the chart into kubememo-runtime:

helm upgrade --install kubememo ./charts/kubememo \
  --namespace kubememo-runtime \
  --create-namespace \
  --set activityCapture.enabled=true \
  --set image.repository=ghcr.io/kubedeckio/kubememo \
  --set image.tag=0.0.1

This installs the CRDs and the optional in-cluster watcher. By default, the watcher writes runtime activity memos into the release namespace.

docs.title: Next step
docs.text:
After the CLI is installed, bootstrap the cluster prerequisites:

kubememo install --enable-runtime-store --install-rbac --output json

docs.title: PowerShell Installation
docs.text: KubeMemo is available as a PowerShell module for teams that prefer PowerShell Gallery installation and PowerShell-friendly command names.

docs.title: Install from PowerShell Gallery
docs.text:
Install-Module -Name KubeMemo -Repository PSGallery -Scope CurrentUser

Import the module:

Import-Module KubeMemo

docs.title: Bootstrap cluster prerequisites
docs.text:
Install the durable store:

Install-KubeMemo

Install with runtime memos enabled:

Install-KubeMemo -EnableRuntimeStore -RuntimeNamespace kubememo-runtime

Install with GitOps-aware checks:

Install-KubeMemo -GitOpsAware -EnableRuntimeStore

docs.title: Typical PowerShell workflow
docs.text:
New-KubeMemo -Title "Orders API warm-up behavior" -Summary "Expected transient 502s after deployment" -Content "Ignore failures for up to 3 minutes after rollout." -Kind Deployment -Namespace prod -Name orders-api -NoteType warning
Show-KubeMemo -Kind Deployment -Namespace prod -Name orders-api
Open-KubeMemoTui

docs.title: How PowerShell fits the product
docs.text: The PowerShell experience uses the native kubememo binary underneath, while keeping PowerShell-friendly command names, terminal passthrough for the TUI, and object-returning behavior where it fits PowerShell workflows.

docs.text:
• Install-KubeMemo
  • Uninstall-KubeMemo
  • Update-KubeMemo
  • Test-KubeMemoInstallation
  • Get-KubeMemoInstallationStatus

docs.text:

docs.title: Write and remove
docs.text:

  • Set-KubeMemo
  • Remove-KubeMemo
  • Clear-KubeMemo

docs.title: GitOps and config
docs.text:
• Export-KubeMemo
  • Import-KubeMemo
  • Sync-KubeMemoGitOps
  • Test-KubeMemoGitOpsMode
  • Test-KubeMemoRuntimeStore
  • Get-KubeMemoConfig
  • Get-KubeMemoActivity
  • Start-KubeMemoActivityCapture

docs.title: Common examples
docs.text:
Get-KubeMemo -Namespace prod
Show-KubeMemo -Kind Deployment -Namespace prod -Name orders-api
New-KubeMemo -Temporary -Title "Investigation" -Summary "Watching rollout" -Content "Temporary runtime note" -Kind Deployment -Namespace prod -Name orders-api -NoteType incident

docs.title: Durable memo CRD
docs.text:
• Kind: Memo
  • Group: notes.kubememo.io
  • Version: v1alpha1
  • Resource: memos
  • Short names: km, memo

docs.title: Runtime memo CRD
docs.text:
• Kind: RuntimeMemo
  • Group: runtime.notes.kubememo.io
  • Version: v1alpha1
  • Resource: runtimememos
  • Short names: kmr, rmemo

docs.title: Examples
docs.text:
kubectl get memos -A
kubectl get runtimememos -A
kubectl get km -A
kubectl get kmr -A
